_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1630 batch)
_meta.note:
VA RICHMOND LOCAL batch — 5 officials (4 city council + 1 RPS school board). ellen-robertson (CC D6 Gateway, D, in office since 2003), katherine-jordan-rva (CC D2 N-Central + Vice President 2025-26, D, since 2021), kenya-gibson (CC D3 Northside, D, sworn 1/1/2025, prior RPS SB D3 2018-2024), matthew-percival (RPS School Board Vice Chair D1, I/nonpartisan, sworn 1/1/2025), nicole-jones-rva (CC D9 South Central, I/nonpartisan, appointed 1/1/2024 to Michael-Jones HD-77 vacancy, elected to full term Nov 2024). KEY EVIDENCE: (1) Robertson — HARD TRUE econ_q2: voted YES on Nov 12 2024 Trammell 4-cent rate cut (from $1.20 to $1.16, failed 5-4) + actively pressured Avula CAO Odie Donald to budget for lower rate ('No ma'am' answer in committee). HARD FALSE bib_marriage q2: 2018 Stoney credit + chpn.net — Robertson advanced SOGI non-discrimination framework + trans-inclusive city employee health benefits, lifting Richmond HRC Municipal Equality Index 52 points. HARD FALSE pj_q1: advanced 2020 defund-RPD committee resolution 2-1, chairs Alternatives to Incarceration Board, founded Office of Community Wealth Building, framed marijuana legalization as helping incarcerated. HARD FALSE pj_q3: in own voice as Council VP May 13 2021 — 'Gun violence is certainly a public crisis... national crisis that calls for us to use all the resources at our disposal to keep people safe' — textbook public-health framing. NOTE: not signatory on 2025 Pride proclamation (Avula + Newbille + Jordan signed; Robertson did not) — bib_marriage q4 NULL not FALSE. Heterodox-D fiscal-restraint signal is the genuine catch this batch. (2) Jordan — HARD FALSE sanctity_q1: voluntarily asked to be ADDED as patron of July 2024 unanimous ordinance transferring former Brook Hill School site to VA League for Planned Parenthood for $6M health center. HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4: signed June 10 2025 'Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community in perpetuity' as Council VP w/ Mayor Avula + Pres Newbille. HARD FALSE econ_q2: voted NO on Nov 12 2024 4-cent rate cut effort; backed Avula 2025 hold-the-rate plan; quoted as VP whip 'We are in this together as a city.' HARD FALSE pj_q3: 'upstream issues' framing + supports social-worker mental-health response model. Climate Emergency Resolution sponsor (July 2021), lone NO on BOTH casino referendums (no rubric cell — 'I realize I'm the outlier' quote). 0 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells. (3) Gibson — HARD FALSE bib_marriage q4: co-presented May 29 2026 Pride Month proclamation w/ Mayor Avula at City Hall flag-raising. HARD FALSE bib_marriage q2: as RPS SB 8-1 vote Oct 3 2022 REJECTED Youngkin trans model policies (only Jonathan Young dissented). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: same Oct 2022 trans-affirming RPS resolution maps to school-board q2 'has voted to remove CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials' — she voted opposite direction. HARD FALSE pj_q0 (HIGH BAR cleared in REVERSE direction): Sept 2021 motion to END RPS MOU with Richmond Police + remove SROs by June 2023 (failed 3-6, but she SPONSORED it). HARD FALSE pj_q3: gun violence framed as 'baked into our society through extreme economic inequality and racist policy' — campaign-site quote in her own voice. HARD FALSE self_defense q1: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction Aug 29 2024. SPARSE-DATA NOTE: economic_stewardship q2 — ABSTAINED on FY26 real estate tax rate (Sept 2025) pending more data on collective-bargaining costs — null per discipline. 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells. RICH dual-tenure record (RPS SB 2018-2024 + CC since 2025) means more scoreable cells than a typical first-term local. (4) Percival — HARD TRUE econ_q2: voted NO on adding $3M Virtual Academy funding (failed 6-2, his minority position); quoted 'An additional $3 million may be a difficult ask in this environment'; voted YES on June 2026 unanimous ~$4M FY27 deficit cuts (incl. teacher-stipend cuts + closing Virtual Academy). Multi-cycle fiscal-restraint signal. HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: campaign platform explicitly opposes parental/governmental review of library content — teachers should not be 'search[ed] for subversive messages' and should have 'freedom to use age-appropriate literature they believe will best prepare students for life beyond graduation' (vpm.org elections Q&A). HARD FALSE border_immigration q1+q2: voted YES Feb 4 2025 UNANIMOUS RPS 'Safe Zone' resolution declaring ICE presence on campuses 'substantially disrupts the learning environment' and limiting agent admission — sanctuary-style ICE access restrictions on school grounds. HARD FALSE refuse_state_overreach q3 (subsidiarity): April 2025 budget discussion — explicitly worried about RPS being trapped on 'Comprehensive Support and Improvement' status IF Trump dismantles US DoE; posture is pro-federal-DoE-oversight, opposite of subsidiarity. SYMBOLIC FLAG (not scored): took oath Jan 8 2025 with NO BOOK (other members used Quran, Constitution, family Bibles). Net 1 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells. Charter-skeptical via Richmonder quote noted in profile, not scored on family q0 absent direct anti-choice vote. (5) Nicole Jones — HARD FALSE econ_q2: voted NO on Oct 14 2025 rate-cut proposal ($1.20→$1.16, failed 3-5-1); quoted 'We don't have nothing to show that by changing this rate or the rate that we keep is actually going to get us to where we need to be.' SPARSE-DATA OUTCOME: only ~17 mo on council, deliberately narrow policy lane, all 29 other rubric cells null. Restorative-justice + 'community justice and systemic thinking' campaign framing noted in profile but no specific cashless-bail / Soros-DA / decarceral VOTE located → public_justice q1 null per high-bar discipline. NVM + SEIU + Bagby + ProgVotersGuide D-coalition alignment confirmed. 0 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells. Fix party Unknown→I.

RUNBOOK BLOCKER STATUS (this batch DEFERRED 4 records that the picker keeps surfacing): (a) rick-west VA Mayor Chesapeake — 5TH consecutive defer; slug-collision with rick-west OK State Rep persists, refine-records.py by_slug last-wins; research stashed in 2026-06-02 0950 dossier; would score 67/100 with 6 cited cells. NEEDS slug-rename helper (proposed rename: rick-west VA → rick-west-chesapeake; preserve OK record). (b)+(c)+(d) vernon-tillage + william-dodson + yolanda-thomas — already research-complete with confidence=roster_verified per 1625 Portsmouth batch; runbook picker uses 'not confidence.startswith("evidence")' filter which excludes roster_verified, so they keep being surfaced. NEEDS filter widening to accept roster_verified as 'done' OR promote-to-evidence_local-with-all-nulls convention. Both blockers should be addressed by a one-time engine fix before the next ~5 LOCAL VA batches. This batch picked the next 5 fresh candidates (Richmond) to avoid burning 4 of 5 batch slots on blocked records — a deliberate departure from the runbook's literal STEP-2 query.

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no church affiliation found on her campaign site, council bio, LinkedIn, or media coverage reviewed. (RULED OUT: 'Co-Pastor Elena Robertson' at Mt. Gilead Full Gospel International Ministries is a different person — different first name and bio.)
profile.education: B.S. Urban Studies and Planning, Virginia Commonwealth University; A.A.S., J. Sargeant Reynolds Community College; LPN certificate, Richmond Technical Center School of Nursing.
profile.background: Founder of a community housing-development corporation that provided down-payment grants to 300+ low/moderate-income first-time homebuyers. First African-American female Chair of Richmond's Planning Commission before joining Council. First elected to Richmond City Council, District 6 (Gateway), Nov 5 2003; sworn in Jan 2004. Re-elected Nov 5 2024 with 53.3% (vs Willie Hilliard Jr. 31.6% and former aide Tavares Floyd 14.4%); current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot, next election 2028. Has previously served as Council Vice President. Resident of the North Highland Park neighborhood.

ellen-robertson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 6 (Gateway), serving continuously since Jan 2004 (sworn after Nov 2003 election); re-elected Nov 5 2024 53.3% three-way race; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. HARD TRUE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on Nov 12 2024 Trammell-proposed real estate tax rate cut from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 5-4; Robertson with Trammell/Lambert/Lynch in the YES minority); actively pressured the Avula administration in committee for a lower-rate baseline budget — quoted asking CAO Odie Donald II whether the administration would build the budget around a lower rate if Council asked; Donald replied 'No ma'am' and Robertson pushed back suggesting $16M in prior-year surplus could absorb the $3.2M revenue hit. Heterodox-D fiscal-restraint signal — the cleanest cross-party catch this batch (parallel to Trammell/Lambert/Lynch faction against the Newbille/Avula hold-the-rate coalition). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: Mayor Levar Stoney publicly credited Robertson (with then-Councilmember Parker Agelasto) as 'key in moving Richmond's Human Rights Commission and non-discrimination laws forward in the city council,' which produced the city's SOGI non-discrimination framework AND 'transgender-inclusive health benefits for city employees' that lifted Richmond's HRC Municipal Equality Index score 52 points (chpn.net 2018) — affirmative advocacy on the trans-affirming-policy-for-city-employees direction, the rubric's adverse direction on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: voted with then-Councilmember Mike Jones in committee 2-1 to advance the 2020 police-defunding study resolution to full council with a recommendation to approve (wric.com); founded Richmond's Office of Community Wealth Building (root-causes-of-poverty framing); chairs the city's Alternatives to Incarceration Board; on the Northam marijuana-legalization-amendments advance she framed legalization as supporting 'people who are incarcerated for possession' and 'help[ing] people seek health assistance' (wric.com) — explicit decarceral / soft-on-crime direction across multiple vectors. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: in her own voice as Council Vice President, May 13 2021 gun-violence-prevention rally — 'Gun violence is certainly a public crisis that extends beyond Richmond; it's a national crisis that calls for us to use all the resources at our disposal to keep people safe' (rva.gov) — textbook public-health/national-crisis framing of violent crime, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 (parallel to Doyle 'rec centers / libraries / community groups' framing scored FALSE in the 1550 Norfolk batch). NOT scored biblical_marriage q4 (Pride proclamations): research confirms Robertson was NOT a signatory on the June 10 2025 Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community (Avula + Newbille + Jordan signed; Robertson did not) — null not FALSE per strict cite-only discipline (absence of signature is not affirmative opposition; she also has not publicly dissented). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): Robertson said 'Richmond needs a police department. I am open to exploring concerns and addressing them' on the 2020 defund proposal but did not make an affirmative pro-police defense of the chief or oppose-defund advocacy; her actual committee vote ADVANCED the defund study. Null on q0 + the FALSE on q1 captures the soft-on-crime posture without overreach. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / industry_capture / public_justice q2 + q4 — no quote-bearing positions located in 22 years of service despite the long council tenure (sparse cultural-issue record on a fiscally-mixed urban progressive). 1 TRUE / 3 FALSE on cited cells = 25% — coherent center-left urban D council member whose distinctive feature is heterodox fiscal restraint (Trammell-coalition tax-cut yes-vote + active administration pressure for a lower-rate budget). Recommend revisiting if Robertson signs a future Pride proclamation, takes a position on the 2025 Avula sanctuary stance, or after the FY27 budget cycle.

2.note: Mayor Stoney publicly credited Robertson (with then-Councilmember Parker Agelasto) as 'key in moving Richmond's Human Rights Commission and non-discrimination laws forward in the city council,' which produced the city's SOGI non-discrimination framework AND 'transgender-inclusive health benefits for city employees' that lifted Richmond's HRC Municipal Equality Index score 52 points (2018). Her campaign-site self-description 'all of my policies and services are governed to be inclusive' corroborates the trans-affirming-policy-for-city-employees posture (rubric-adverse on q2).

Voted YES on Nov 12 2024 Trammell-proposed real estate tax rate cut from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 5-4; Robertson with Trammell, Lambert, and Lynch in the YES minority). In committee actively pressured CAO Odie Donald II to build the Avula budget around a lower baseline rate ('No ma'am' answer); pushed back suggesting $16M in prior-year surplus could absorb the $3.2M revenue hit. Multi-vector heterodox-D fiscal restraint.

1.note: Voted with then-Councilmember Mike Jones in committee 2-1 to ADVANCE the 2020 police-defunding study resolution to full council with a recommendation to approve. Founded Richmond's Office of Community Wealth Building (root-causes-of-poverty framing). Chairs the city's Alternatives to Incarceration Board. On marijuana-legalization said legalization 'will support people who are incarcerated for possession and will help people seek health assistance' — explicit decarceral / soft-on-crime direction on multiple independent vectors (rubric-adverse on q1).

3.note: Council Vice President Robertson, May 13 2021 gun-violence-prevention rally, in her own voice: 'Gun violence is certainly a public crisis that extends beyond Richmond; it's a national crisis that calls for us to use all the resources at our disposal to keep people safe.' Textbook public-health / national-crisis framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3, parallel to Doyle 1550 Norfolk-batch FALSE).

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no church affiliation found on her campaign site, council bio, LinkedIn, or media coverage reviewed. (She was born at St. Mary's Hospital, a Catholic hospital, but that is biographical not affirmative current-faith profession.)
profile.education: Graduate degree in Urban & Environmental Planning, University of Virginia. Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership (2010); Leadership Metro Richmond (2006).
profile.background: Richmond native; parents history professors at VCU and University of Richmond. Career: Project Manager at Cooper, Robertson & Partners (NYC architecture/urban-design firm); Assistant Director for Campus Design & Sustainability at Duke University Health System (master planning $700M new construction across 240 acres). Lives in the Fan neighborhood with her husband and two daughters. Prior civic record: Fan District Association president; Richmond Green City Commission member; RPS parent advocate; member of the VAratifyERA bipartisan leadership team. Won the 2020 Richmond City Council District 2 (North Central) race; sworn Jan 4 2021; re-elected unopposed Nov 5 2024; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot, next election 2028. Elected Council Vice President for 2025-2026.

katherine-jordan-rva.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council 2nd Voter District (North Central), sworn Jan 4 2021; re-elected unopposed Nov 5 2024; current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Council Vice President 2025-2026 (rvahub.com Jan 2025 organizational meeting). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed (born at St. Mary's Hospital is biographical not affirmative). EDUCATION: UVa Urban & Environmental Planning grad degree. HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q1: voluntarily asked to be ADDED as a patron of the July 2024 ordinance unanimously approving transfer of the former Brook Hill School site to the Virginia League for Planned Parenthood for a $6M health center (richmondbizsense.com 7/23/2024); joined patrons Addison, Lambert, Jones, Nye, Lynch, and Stoney. Explicit affirmative facilitation of a PP clinic land-transfer on city-controlled property — rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('refused to declare sanctuary city for abortion' / pro-life city resolutions). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: signed the June 10 2025 'Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community' with Mayor Avula and Council President Newbille, declaring Richmond an 'official Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community, in perpetuity' and a 'safe place for LGBTQ+ people to live their lives freely, openly, authentically' — signed in her capacity as Council Vice President (rva.gov 6/10/2025). Affirmative Pride proclamation, the cleanest hit on q4 ('opposes city/county Pride proclamations' inverse). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the Nov 12 2024 proposed real estate tax rate reduction from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (vpm.org 11/12/2024 + richmondfreepress.com 11/14/2024); in 2025 backed Avula's plan to keep the rate the same when the cut was proposed again; as VP whipped the council on the FY26 $3B budget with the 'We are in this together as a city' line (richmondbizsense.com 5/13/2025). Three-vector vote-against-restraint posture — rubric-adverse on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: 2020 Richmond Magazine Q&A framed gun violence and homelessness around 'upstream issues' like living wages and wraparound services rather than emergency responses; supports a social-worker mental-health-call response model that displaces police on mental-health calls — explicit root-causes / public-health framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3). NOT scored public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR not cleared): she stated 'broader public safety approach that includes social workers for mental health and substance abuse calls' and 'All departments, including police, must justify funding requests during fiscal constraints' (Richmond Magazine 2020) and supports civilian review board with enforcement power — these are governance-neutral or LE-skeptical, NOT affirmative pro-police defense, so null per discipline rather than FALSE (which would require an affirmative anti-LE vote that didn't surface). NOT scored christian_liberty q3: the 2021 restoration of tax-exempt status to Community Church of God in Christ (richmondfreepress.com 7/1/2021) could be relevant but no individually-attributed Jordan vote / quote located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q0 / biblical_marriage q0+q2 / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty other cells / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / industry_capture / public_justice q1+q2+q4 — no quote-bearing positions located. ADDITIONAL CONTEXTUAL MARKERS: sponsored Richmond's Climate and Ecological Emergency Resolution July 2021 (commonwealthtimes.org 9/29/2021) — ESG-direction signal but no specific local rubric cell maps cleanly; lone NO on BOTH Richmond casino referendums with quote 'I realize I'm the outlier. That's not a role I like, but I think we all have to vote our conscience' (richmonder.org) — principled-dissent flag but no rubric cell maps. Endorsed by Sierra Club, Home Builders Association, Richmond Association of Realtors, and Rep. Jennifer McClellan (D-VA-04) in 2024. 0 TRUE / 4 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent progressive-D council vice president with PP facilitation + Pride proclamation signatory + anti-tax-cut + public-health-crime framing. Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle.

1.note: Voluntarily asked to be ADDED as a patron of the July 2024 ordinance unanimously approving transfer of the former Brook Hill School site to the Virginia League for Planned Parenthood for a $6M health center; joined patrons Addison, Lambert, Jones, Nye, Lynch, Stoney. Affirmative facilitation of a PP clinic land-transfer on city-controlled property (rubric-adverse on local q1 'refused to declare sanctuary city for abortion' / pro-life city resolutions inverse).

4.note: Signed the June 10 2025 'Joint Proclamation: Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community' with Mayor Avula and Council President Newbille, declaring Richmond an 'official Richmond LGBTQ+ Friendly Community, in perpetuity' and a 'safe place for LGBTQ+ people to live their lives freely, openly, authentically.' Signed in her capacity as Council Vice President. Affirmative Pride proclamation signatory (rubric-adverse on q4).

2.note: Voted NO on the Nov 12 2024 proposed real estate tax rate reduction from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100. In 2025 backed Mayor Avula's plan to keep the rate the same when the cut was proposed again. As Vice President whipped the council on the FY26 $3B budget with 'We are in this together as a city.' Three-vector vote-against-restraint posture on the local fiscal cell (rubric-adverse on q2 in the Norfolk-batch precedent that maps 'balanced budgets' to rate restraint).

3.note: Richmond Magazine Q&A: framed homelessness/safety around 'upstream issues' like living wages and wraparound services rather than only emergency responses; supports a social-worker response model for mental-health calls. Explicit root-causes / public-health framing of violent crime in her own voice (rubric-adverse on q3, parallel to Robertson/Doyle FALSEs).

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — not disclosed on her campaign site, Ballotpedia, VPAP, LinkedIn, or any news coverage reviewed.
profile.background: Marketing/advertising career across The Disney Store (2001-03), MGA Entertainment (2003-06), Circuit City (2006), PowerPact (2007-09), Director of Client Services at Franklin Street Marketing (2009-14), Marketing Strategy Director at ByteJam (2014-16), Account Director at Epiphany Studio RVA (2017-21), and Director of Digital Marketing at Acoustical Solutions (recent). Single working mother of two RPS students; former PTA vice-president. Served on Richmond Public Schools School Board, District 3 (2018-2024; first elected 2017, re-elected 2020). Defeated incumbent Ann-Frances Lambert and Maria Carra Rose in the Nov 5 2024 general election for Richmond City Council District 3 (Northside); sworn Jan 1 2025; current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

kenya-gibson.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council District 3 (Northside), sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Prior service on Richmond Public Schools School Board District 3 (2018-2024). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. EDUCATION: William & Mary B.A. RICH DUAL-TENURE RECORD: prior RPS SB service (2018-2024) adds scoreable family_child_sovereignty + biblical_marriage cells beyond a typical first-term council member. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: as sitting councilmember Gibson co-presented Richmond's 2026 Pride Month Proclamation with Mayor Avula at the May 29 2026 City Hall Pride flag-raising ceremony (wvtf.org 5/29/2026 + richmonder.org). Affirmative Pride proclamation participation (rubric-adverse on q4). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: as Richmond School Board D3 member Gibson was one of 8 board members to vote YES on the Oct 3 2022 resolution REJECTING Gov. Youngkin's model transgender student policies and affirming protections for students 'regardless of sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression' (newsweek 10/3/2022 + rvahub 10/4/2022); vote was 8-1, only Jonathan Young dissented, Gibson among the 8 affirmatives — the rubric-adverse direction on q2 ('opposes city/county trans-affirming policies' inverse). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: same Oct 2022 RPS vote maps directly to the local family q2 cell ('school board has voted to REMOVE CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials' inverse) — she voted to RETAIN/AFFIRM the trans-affirming gender-identity framework. HARD FALSE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared in REVERSE direction): as RPS SB member Sept 2021, Gibson introduced the MOTION to end RPS's MOU with Richmond Police and remove School Resource Officers by June 2023, replacing them with 'community-sourced safety professionals' + restorative-justice task force (vpm.org 10/5/2021 + wric.com); motion failed 3-6 but she SPONSORED it. RVA Mag op-ed corroborates 'she argued for the removal of police from schools.' As current councilmember she also opposes the Flock license-plate-reader contract (vpm.org 5/11/2026 + 5/20/2026). Multi-vector affirmative anti-LE advocacy — rubric-adverse on q0. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: campaign site quote in her own voice — gun violence is 'baked into our society through extreme economic inequality and racist policy' requiring anti-poverty spending and gun restrictions; cites firearm deaths as 'leading cause of mortality for children and teens'; earned Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction (Aug 29 2024, kenyagibson.com updates). Explicit public-health / structural-inequality framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3). HARD FALSE self_defense q1: Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction Aug 29 2024 for commitments on gun restrictions and gun control at state/federal level — direct gun-control endorsement on the rubric-adverse direction. NOT scored economic_stewardship q2: ABSTAINED on FY26 real estate tax rate Sept 2025 asking for more data on collective-bargaining costs (vpm.org 9/23/2025); did not affirmatively push to cut or hold the rate — null per discipline. RVA Mag op-ed states Gibson 'pushed for progressive taxation — raising taxes on the wealthy' but no specific sponsored Richmond-council tax-increase ordinance located, so the FALSE-direction signal is suggestive not vote-bearing. NOT scored border_immigration q1/q2: Richmond as a body has long-standing 1990 sanctuary ordinance and Mayor Avula publicly opposes 287(g) cooperation, but no individually-attributed Gibson sponsored-vote on sanctuary status located — null per discipline. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / family_child_sovereignty q0+q1 / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / industry_capture / public_justice q1+q2+q4 / self_defense q0 — no quote-bearing positions located despite the dual-tenure record. ENDORSEMENT COALITION: NVM, SEIU VA State Council (incl. 32BJ + 512), LiUNA Local 804, Richmond Education Association, Teamsters Local 322, Sunrise Movement Richmond, Richmond DSA, Richmond for All, The Collective PAC, Lead Locally (2024 Green New Deal Slate), Moms Demand Action — uniformly progressive-D coalition. 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent progressive-D council member with rich RPS SB history backing the school-board family/marriage cells (Oct 2022 trans resolution + Sept 2021 SRO removal motion). Recommend revisiting after FY27 budget cycle and any 2027 cultural-issue council votes.

2.note: As Richmond Public Schools School Board D3 member, Gibson voted YES on the Oct 3 2022 resolution REJECTING Gov. Youngkin's model transgender student policies and affirming protections for students 'regardless of sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression.' 8-1 vote; only Jonathan Young (D4) dissented, Gibson among the 8 affirmatives. Rubric-adverse direction on local q2 ('opposes city/county trans-affirming policies' inverse).

4.note: As sitting councilmember Gibson co-presented Richmond's 2026 Pride Month Proclamation with Mayor Danny Avula at the May 29 2026 City Hall Pride flag-raising ceremony. Affirmative Pride proclamation participation (rubric-adverse on q4).

2.note: Same Oct 3 2022 RPS School Board 8-1 vote — directly affirmed the trans-affirming gender-identity framework in district policy, the inverse of local family q2 ('school board has voted to remove CRT/SOGI/comprehensive-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials').

0.note: As RPS SB member Sept 2021, Gibson INTRODUCED the motion to end RPS's MOU with Richmond Police and remove School Resource Officers by June 2023, replacing them with 'community-sourced safety professionals' + restorative-justice task force (failed 3-6 but she sponsored it). RVA Mag corroborates 'she argued for the removal of police from schools.' As current councilmember opposes Flock license-plate-reader police contract. Multi-vector affirmative anti-LE advocacy — HIGH-BAR-cleared FALSE direction on q0 ('publicly backed law enforcement and opposed defunding/dismantling police' inverse).

3.note: Campaign site quote in her own voice: gun violence is 'baked into our society through extreme economic inequality and racist policy' requiring anti-poverty spending and gun restrictions; cites firearm deaths as 'leading cause of mortality for children and teens'; framing committed publicly via Moms Demand Action Gun Sense Candidate distinction announcement. Explicit public-health / structural-inequality framing of violent crime (rubric-adverse on q3).

1.note: Earned Moms Demand Action 'Gun Sense Candidate' distinction Aug 29 2024 for her commitments on gun restrictions and gun control at the state/federal level (with red-flag / background-check / safe-storage policy alignment that the Moms Demand Action vetting requires). Direct gun-control endorsement on the rubric-adverse direction on q1 ('opposes local-ordinance red-flag/magazine/AWB analogs' inverse).

profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — campaign committee, RPS bio, and media coverage do not disclose a denomination or church. SYMBOLIC FLAG: at Jan 8 2025 swearing-in took the oath of office with NO BOOK (other board members used Quran, U.S. Constitution, and family Bibles per Richmonder coverage). Not dispositive of religion but evidence he is not a publicly-affirming Christian-identity officeholder.
profile.education: Not publicly itemized in reviewed sources beyond U.S. State Department information-security engineering career background.
profile.background: RPS parent 10+ years; PTA volunteer at his children's RPS schools across the district for over a decade. Career federal IT — information-security engineer at the U.S. Department of State. Previously applied (one of 10 candidates) to fill the District 1 RPS School Board seat in 2016 when Glen Sturtevant left for the VA Senate; lost the 2016 appointment to Elizabeth Doerr. Replaced Doerr by winning unopposed in the Nov 5 2024 RPS School Board District 1 election; sworn Jan 1 2025; term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot. Elected School Board Vice Chair by unanimous roll-call at the Jan 8 2025 organizational meeting (Shavonda Fernandez elected Chair). VA city school-board races are formally nonpartisan; he has not registered or run as D or R. Listed on bluevoterguide.org as a 'Virginia Nonpartisan Candidate'; endorsed by REGS (Richmonders for Effective Governance of Schools) in Oct 2024.

matthew-percival.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond Public Schools School Board Vice Chair, District 1, sworn 1/1/2025, term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Fix party Unknown→I (formally nonpartisan ballot; no public D/R registration confirmed). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed; symbolic Jan 8 2025 oath taken with NO BOOK (richmonder.org). EDUCATION/CAREER: U.S. State Department information-security engineer + 10+ years RPS PTA. profile.religion: NO PUBLIC EVIDENCE FOUND — not disclosed in campaign site, Ballotpedia, VPAP, or media coverage reviewed.
profile.education: Education credentials not publicly itemized in reviewed sources.
profile.background: New York native, Richmond resident 25+ years. Deputy Director, ART 180 (Richmond youth arts nonprofit). Prior career in business development/management (~20+ years) including work at state agencies including the Library of Virginia. Mother of two RPS students; co-founded a neighborhood civic association. Served on Richmond Public Schools School Board, District 9 (2020 election; served 2021-2023). Appointed interim to Richmond City Council, South Central 9th Voter District, on Dec 12 2023 to fill the vacancy after Michael J. Jones won the HD-77 seat (sworn Jan 1 2024). Elected to full term Nov 5 2024 with 58% (vs Stephanie Starling 41.7%); current term ends Dec 31 2028 — NOT on the Nov 2026 ballot.

nicole-jones-rva.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Richmond City Council South Central 9th Voter District, appointed interim 12/12/2023 (sworn 1/1/2024), elected to full term Nov 5 2024 w/ 58%, current term ends 12/31/2028 — NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Prior service on RPS School Board D9 (2021-2023). Fix party Unknown→I (Richmond CC races are officially nonpartisan per VA code; no formal D/R registration confirmed though coalition is uniformly D-aligned). RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. CAREER: ART 180 nonprofit Deputy Director. REAL-WORLD ALIGNMENT: D-coalition — endorsed by New Virginia Majority, SEIU, state Sen. Lamont Bagby (D), multiple RPS School Board members, predecessor Dr. Michael J. Jones (D, now HD-77), and listed in 2024 Progressive Voters Guide; also endorsed by Richmond Association of Realtors, Richmond Building Trades, Home Building Association of Richmond. No GOP or single-issue conservative endorsements. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted NO on the Oct 14 2025 proposal to cut Richmond real estate tax rate from $1.20 to $1.16 per $100 (failed 3-5-1; Jones in the 5-vote NO majority that defeated the cut). Quoted on the vote: 'We don't have nothing to show that by changing this rate or the rate that we keep is actually going to get us to where we need to be' (12onyourside.com 10/15/2025 + vpm.org 10/15/2025). Direct vote-against-restraint posture — rubric-adverse on q2 (Norfolk-batch precedent maps local q2 to rate restraint). Aligned with Newbille/Avula coalition, NOT the Trammell/Lambert/Lynch fiscal-pushback faction. SPARSE-DATA OUTCOME: ALL 29 OTHER LOCAL CELLS NULL per strict cite-only discipline. Only ~17 mo on council (~24 mo counting interim service); narrow policy lane (housing, basic city services, restorative justice framing); has not surfaced quote-bearing positions on culture-war markers.
